title: MAS to boost Singapore equities market with education, guardrails and a risk‑taking framework
body: MAS deputy chairman Chee Hong Tat announced increased resources for public and investor education, new guardrails and a more risk‑taking approach to revive Singapore’s equities market, while emphasizing transparency, shareholder engagement and stronger enforcement. He also responded to Workers’ Party proposals, noting that some value‑creation disclosures may become mandatory but stressing that not all measures should be prescribed.

title: SGX posts strongest half‑year results since 2000, driven by higher trading volumes and IPO activity
body: SGX reported a half‑year net profit of $342.7 million (up 0.8%) and revenue of $736.2 million (up 7.9%), with daily average securities trading value rising 20% to $1.51 billion—the highest in five years, and an interim dividend of 21.75 cents per share, up 20.8% year‑on‑year. The exchange attributed the performance to stronger multi‑asset trading, a robust IPO pipeline of more than 30 companies and the Equity Market Development Programme supported by the Monetary Authority of Singapore.

title: Yangzijiang Shipbuilding fell about 5.6% after a drop in Maersk earnings and job‑cut plans, pulling Singapore's Straits Times Index down 0.7%
body: Yangzijiang Shipbuilding’s shares slid 5.3% to 5.6% in early trading, erasing over S$700 million in market value, after Maersk reported lower earnings and announced plans to cut 1,000 jobs, with Maersk shares down 3.7% in Copenhagen. The broader market saw the STI down 0.7% and declines in DBS (‑0.7%), OCBC (‑0.9%) and Singtel (‑0.8%), while regional indices and US tech stocks also fell.

title: SGX posts modest profit rise, raises interim dividend and highlights strong IPO pipeline
body: SGX reported H1 FY2026 adjusted net profit of S$357.1 million (up 11.6%) with revenue of S$736.2 million and declared an interim dividend of S$0.11 per share payable on Feb 24. CEO Loh Boon Chye said trading broadened across sectors, institutional investors bought S$415 million of small‑ and mid‑cap stocks, the exchange raised nearly S$3 billion from IPOs and is preparing a Global Listing Board to attract more high‑growth companies.

title: Keppel shares jump as it proposes 13‑cent dividend while M1 stake sale to Simba Telecom faces regulatory delay
body: Keppel's shares rose over 6% after announcing a 13‑cent special dividend, but the expected $1.43 billion sale of its 83.9% stake in M1 to Simba Telecom is delayed pending regulatory approval, with cash proceeds of about $1 billion slated for recognition in the 2026 monetisation value. The company also highlighted the need to boost occupancy at its Keppel South Central office tower before any further divestments and outlined its early‑stage AI initiatives and competitive positioning against firms such as BlackRock and Global Infrastructure Partners.

title: Asian markets slide as AI spending worries hit Singapore’s STI and crypto prices tumble
body: The Straits Times Index fell 0.8% to 4,934.41 as investors reacted to Amazon’s $200 billion AI‑related spending plan and broader tech sell‑off, dragging down blue‑chip stocks such as Yangzijiang Shipbuilding (‑6.2%), DBS (‑0.6%), OCBC (‑1%) and Singtel (‑1.3%). Bitcoin slipped to around US$63,000, a 50% drop over three months, with Maybank analysts linking the crypto decline to rising AI costs and the nomination of hawkish Fed chair candidate Kevin Warsh, while regional indices showed mixed moves.
